slides.title: Your workouts stopped working *for one reason*.
slides.sub: It's not your genetics or your split. The stimulus stopped changing — so your body stopped adapting.

slides.title: Muscle adapts to new demand
slides.body: Your body only builds muscle when a workout is *harder than what it's used to*. Same weights, same reps = a maintenance signal.

slides.title: Add a little, often
slides.body: Progressive overload means slowly doing more: *an extra rep or a little more weight*. That tiny upward pressure is the whole game.

slides.title: You can't beat what you forget
slides.body: Nobody remembers last week's numbers. *Write down your sets and reps* — without a record you can't tell if you're progressing.

slides.title: Soreness isn't the goal
slides.body: Chasing soreness or 'muscle confusion' just adds random fatigue. *Progress beats novelty* — the boring plan you can measure wins.

slides.title: Recover, then push
slides.body: Muscle grows on rest days, not in the gym. *Sleep and protein* are what let next week's numbers actually go up.

slides.title: Add a rep. *Then add another.*
slides.body: Vibe Health helps you track training, protein and sleep together — so progressive overload and recovery actually line up.

caption:
If your workouts stopped working, the stimulus probably stopped changing. 🏋️

Muscle only adapts to more than it's used to — so add a rep or a little weight, write it down, and let sleep and protein cash it in. Boring beats fancy. 🔖

Vibe Health ties your training, protein and sleep together → link in bio.
